body: TUNGSTEN -- 3-agent smithing pass complete (forge-edge-2 + forge-4d-alpha + forge-parallel, no forge-indicate per your instruction). Shipped WARRANT#53 -> SLS(68)/9.82: found that RunComprehensiveGhostValidation already implements a real, comprehensive gate suite (WR/PF/EV/MaxConsecLoss, frequency, z-test significance, 8-window walk-forward, volatility-stress test, weight-stability) -- this IS the real machinery forge-edge-2s undefined S1-S5 language was always describing. Mapped S1-S5 onto it honestly (not invented from scratch) and wired a REAL ForgeScorecard from its actual output for the first time -- running it through the ratchet decision (ForgeRatchetPromote). PBO and deflated-Sharpe remain honest placeholders since no code anywhere computes them yet -- the ratchet will correctly refuse to promote anything through this path until a real PBO statistic exists, which is the safe direction to be wrong in: the pipeline is now real and wired end-to-end, even though nothing will visibly promote yet. Compiled clean on the first attempt, diff-verified -- pure addition, zero existing gate logic touched. Also confirmed definitively (a real, standing question closed): ProbabilityDatabase has ZERO persistence anywhere in this codebase -- it is rebuilt from scratch every calibration and every learned pattern is discarded on restart. Did not attempt to build persistence this pass -- properly serializing 1000 pattern slots (each with 3x 14-element sub-arrays) deserves its own careful pass, not a rushed one. Backed up SLS(68) + ledger to x_strategies. This is real progress toward capability for high performance -- the validation/promotion pipeline is now genuinely wired to real data for the first time -- but the underlying signal-quality work (forge-indicate territory) remains the other half of the picture, deliberately untouched this pass per your instruction.
